executive_summary: Your amber rating hinges entirely on supplier turbulence, split between two urgent but distinct problems. First, GPI (USA, printed packaging, critical BAT exposure) has crashed 5.5% without confirmed cause—this needs immediate escalation to understand if it's operational, financial, or market-driven, given the stock move and your high exposure. Second, you have five critical-to-medium BAT exposure suppliers concentrated in China (Huizhou BYD, Smoore, EVE Energy, IP Sun, Porton) all flagged for identical US-China trade and Taiwan risks; these move together, so a single trade escalation or export control tightening could cascade across EMS, batteries, and nicotine sourcing simultaneously. Your packaging tier shows a secondary concern: AMCOR (Switzerland, -2.0%) and GPI (USA, -5.5%) both unexplained, suggesting possible sector-wide headwinds rather than isolated issues—validate whether paper or ink costs spiked, demand shifted, or both suppliers share a raw material pinch. Geopolitical risks on Stora Enso (Finland/Russia border), Sappi (South Africa energy crisis), and Tae Young (Korea/North Korea) are real but slower-moving; prioritize the China cluster and GPI recon this week, then review structural hedging for packaging in parallel.
